plant_parts.name: aerial parts
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita aerial parts, also known as chamomile flowers, have been traditionally used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The essential oil extracted from the aerial parts is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to its calming effects. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in the aerial parts are also known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.

plant_parts.name: flowers
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita flowers are used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. They contain essential oils such as bisabolol and chamazulene, which contribute to their soothing and calming effects. These properties make chamomilla recutita flowers a popular treatment for skin irritations and digestive issues.

plant_parts.name: essential oil
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita essential oil is known for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, often used to calm skin irritations and reduce anxiety. The oil is rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have sedative effects and help with sleep quality. It is commonly used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.

plant_parts.name: flower heads
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita flower heads are a valuable medicinal part of the plant, traditionally used to soothe anxiety and restlessness due to their apigenin and luteolin content. The essential oil extracted from the flower heads contains anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate digestive issues and promote relaxation. Chamomilla recutita flower heads have also been used to treat skin irritations and allergic reactions, reducing redness and promoting wound healing.

plant_parts.name: petals
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita petals are used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The apigenin present in these petals has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it useful for treating anxiety and insomnia. Additionally, chamomilla recutita petals have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and menstrual cramps due to their antispasmodic properties.

plant_parts.name: seeds
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita seeds are not commonly used in herbal medicine, unlike the flowers of the plant. However, the seeds of Chamomilla recutita do contain apigenin, a flavonoid with potential medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal benefits and uses of Chamomilla recutita seeds.

plant_parts.name: floral buds
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita floral buds are used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The buds also possess a mild sedative effect, making them useful in calming anxiety and promoting relaxation. Additionally, the flavonoids present in the buds have been shown to exhibit antioxidant properties, protecting against cell damage and promoting overall well-being.

plant_parts.name: dried flowers
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita dried flowers are traditionally used to calm digestive issues and soothe menstrual cramps due to their anti-inflammatory properties. The dried flowers of this herb are also used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Additionally, they have antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infections and promote wound healing.

plant_parts.name: stems
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita stems are not commonly used for medicinal purposes. However, the leaves and flowers of the plant are used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and calming properties. The essential oil extracted from the flowers is often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.

plant_parts.name: inflorescence
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita inflorescence, which is the flower head of the plant, contains apigenin and luteolin, flavonoids with anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oil present in the inflorescence has been used to treat digestive issues and promote relaxation. It also possesses antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties, making it beneficial for soothing skin irritations and calming the nervous system.

plant_parts.name: leaves
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita leaves are not typically considered medicinal; however, the flowers and essential oil are the primary medicinal parts of the plant. The flowers of Chamomilla recutita are used to create herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, and are valued for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

plant_parts.name: calyx
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita calyx is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and contribute to its medicinal value. The calyx of Chamomilla recutita is also a source of apigenin, a flavonoid known for its calming and soothing effects. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including insomnia, anxiety, and digestive problems.

plant_parts.name: stamens
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita stamens are a lesser-known medicinal part of the plant, often used in herbal remedies for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. They are traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The stamens of Chamomilla recutita are also known for their calming and soothing effects, making them a popular ingredient in teas and tinctures.

plant_parts.name: bracts
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita bracts are used in traditional medicine due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which make them effective in soothing irritated skin and reducing swelling. The bracts of the chamomile plant contain terpenoids and flavonoids that contribute to their medicinal value. In herbal remedies, chamomile bracts are often used to calm digestive issues and promote relaxation.

plant_parts.name: roots
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita roots are not typically used in herbal medicine, as the plant's medicinal properties are usually associated with its flowers and leaves. However, some studies suggest that the roots may contain small amounts of apigenin, a compound with potential anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of chamomilla recutita roots.

plant_parts.name: phyllodes
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita phyllodes, also known as chamomile leaves, contain essential oils such as apigenin and luteolin, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The phyllodes of Chamomilla recutita have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including insomnia, anxiety, and digestive problems. Additionally, the essential oils present in the phyllodes have been shown to have a soothing effect on the skin and can be used to treat minor wounds and irritations.

plant_parts.name: rhizomes
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita rhizomes, also known as German chamomile rhizomes, have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. They contain compounds like chamazulene and farnesene, which have been shown to have potential in reducing inflammation and fighting bacterial infections. The rhizomes are often used topically to treat minor skin irritations and wounds.

plant_parts.name: stigma
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita stigma is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in soothing skin irritations and wounds. The apetalous stigma of chamomilla recutita has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including anxiety and insomnia. Additionally, it has been found to exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall health.

plant_parts.name: apex
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita apex, also known as the flower head of the chamomile plant, contains a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids. The essential oils, particularly bisabolol and chamazulene, have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making the apex useful for treating skin irritations and calming digestive issues. The flavonoids present in the apex have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its medicinal and therapeutic applications.

plant_parts.name: base
plant_parts.desc: Chamomilla recutita base is a valuable component of herbal medicine, primarily consisting of the roots and rhizomes. The root of the plant is rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The rhizome, or underground stem, of chamomilla recutita base is also used medicinally, containing sesquiterpene lactones that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activity.
